reasoning: The March 11, 2025 general election in Greenland resulted in a change of government, with the pro-independence party of former PM Múte Bourup Egede (who had hinted at an early referendum) losing power to 'gradualist' parties. The new unity government, led by Jens-Frederik Nielsen and taking office in April 2025, explicitly postponed the prospect of an independence referendum beyond the 2025-2029 parliamentary term. Major news sources directly following the election confirmed no referendum had been announced. Given the new government's official policy to delay the vote for at least four years, there is no credible chance that an official date for a referendum was scheduled before the July 1, 2025 deadline.
